title: 1M-token context window for large-codebase Prometheus and review scans
source: Anthropic 1M context beta for Sonnet (context-1m) — docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/context-windows; live fetch blocked this run
description: Prometheus scans and code reviews over large repos currently must chunk or sample files to fit the standard context window, which loses cross-file signal (architecture drift, duplicated logic, security patterns spanning modules). Sonnet's 1M-token context lets a single scan ingest a whole subsystem at once, producing higher-quality, more holistic proposals.
proposed_changes: Add an opt-in `--context-1m` flag (and config toggle) that selects Sonnet with the 1M-context beta enabled for whole-subsystem scans. Add a token-budget estimator that switches a scan into 1M mode when the assembled file set exceeds the standard window instead of truncating. Track the higher long-context pricing tier separately in the cost breakdown and log which scans used 1M mode.
